name: Low Country Oyster Loaf
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the bread in half lengthwise; scoop out the soft center of the lower half and set aside for use in other dishes.
2. Place both halves of the loaf, cut side up, on a cookie sheet.
3. Melt the butter in a small saucepan and blend in onion, thyme, basil, and paprika.
4. Brush 2/3 of the butter mixture over the cut sides of the bread, covering completely.
5. Bake at 350 degrees for 15 minutes or until slightly toasted.
6. Fill the lower half of the bread with Oyster Filling; cover with the top half and brush with the remaining seasoned butter.
7. Bake an additional 5 to 10 minutes.
8. Cut into 6 diagonal pieces; garnish each piece with parsley and a wedge of lemon.
9. Serve hot.
Oyster Filling:
1. Rinse oysters in cold water and drain.
2. Mix egg, salt, and pepper in a small bowl.
3. Dip oysters into the egg mixture, then coat with cracker crumbs.
4. Saute oysters in butter until golden brown.
